name: US 10-Year Treasury Yield
summary: Day 71 Saturday. **US 10Y 4.36% MAY 8 CLOSE (-0.6% 1D, +1.7% 1M, +4.0% 3M); US 2Y 3.60% (-0.1% 1D, +0.2% 1M).** Modest 10Y pullback on stronger-than-expected May 8 jobs print (per Reuters/Investing 'labor market resilience') reduced immediate June-cut probability; longer-end yields easing back from FOMC-driven highs. **Real-yield channel still keeping gold underperforming:** US 10Y at 4.36% kept real yields competitive vs gold; gold $4,720.40 (+0.4% 1D) underperformed on a crisis-day with May 7 destroyer attacks + May 8 second tanker disabling + Mokhber 'atomic-bomb' speech. **Cross-asset confirmation of inflation-not-systemic-stress regime:** VIX cycle-low 17.08 + S&P 500 record close 7,398.93 + 10Y not making new highs all reinforce that markets see the West Asia crisis as an INFLATION + FLOW event (negative for gold via real-yield channel) NOT a financial-system stress event (which would be positive for gold + bid for 10Y). Cheniere $3.5B Q1 GAAP loss on derivative MtM is the closest thing to a financial-stress signal but is firm-specific not systemic. India 10Y G-Sec ~7.0% (+6-8 bps cycle, ~+15bps from late-Apr lows, ~+25bps from pre-crisis) — rupee weakness + oil inflation + FII channel keeping pressure. DXY 97.84 (-0.4% 1D, -1.0% 1M) modest dollar weakness. Powell verbal 'hasn't even peaked yet' on energy inflation still operative; CME FedWatch June-cut probability now low.

current.regime: Inflation + flow event regime confirmed (NOT systemic-stress event). Real yields competitive vs gold (gold underperforming on crisis days). May 8 jobs print stronger than expected reduced June-cut probability. Cheniere derivative loss firm-specific not systemic. India 10Y +6-8 bps cycle on rupee/oil/FII pressure.
